# servicenow_mid_server (Resource)

`servicenow_mid_server` manages a MID server (ECC agent) record in ServiceNow. MID servers are normally registered by the MID server installer; create here only to manage existing records or placeholders.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manages a MID server (ECC agent) record in ServiceNow.
#
# WARNING: MID server agents are normally installed on a host using the official
# ServiceNow MID server installer, which auto-registers the record with the
# instance. Creating a record purely through Terraform produces a placeholder row
# with no live agent backing it. Prefer the `servicenow_mid_server` data source
# to reference a MID server that the installer has already registered.

resource "servicenow_mid_server" "example" {
  name        = "example-mid-server"
  host_name   = "mid-host-01.example.com"
  description = "Placeholder MID server created via Terraform"
  agent_type  = "MIDServer"
  validated   = false
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the MID server record.

### Optional

- `agent_type` (String) Type/category of the MID server (maps to ServiceNow's type column).
- `description` (String) Description of the MID server. ServiceNow may or may not surface this column depending on customisations.
- `host_name` (String) Host name the MID server runs on.
- `last_refresh` (String) Date/time of the last refresh from the running MID server agent.
- `linux_user_name` (String) Linux user name the MID server runs under. May not be present on every ServiceNow instance.
- `mid_user` (String) Logged-in user account the MID service runs under (maps to ServiceNow's user_name column).
- `os_name` (String) Operating system distribution running the MID server agent (maps to ServiceNow's host_os_distribution column).
- `os_version` (String) Operating system version on the MID server host (maps to ServiceNow's host_os_version column).
- `started` (String) Date/time the MID server agent was last started.
- `status` (String) Operational status of the MID server (typically computed from MID heartbeat).
- `validated` (Boolean) If set to 'true', the MID server has been validated.
- `version` (String) Version string reported by the running MID server agent.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_mid_server.example <sys_id>
```
